analysis_metadata.paper_title: Observation of a New Particle in the Search for the Standard Model Higgs Boson with the ATLAS Detector at the LHC
analysis_metadata.luminosity: 4.8 fb^-1 at 7 TeV and 5.8 fb^-1 at 8 TeV
analysis_metadata.notes: Extraction restricted to the H→ZZ(*)→4ℓ portion of the combined Higgs discovery paper. Results are for the dedicated four-lepton channel, using the combined 7 TeV and 8 TeV datasets unless otherwise noted.

analysis_objectives.target_process: Standard Model Higgs boson production with H→ZZ(*)→4ℓ
analysis_objectives.description: Search for a Standard Model Higgs boson in the four-lepton final state over the mass range 110–600 GeV using high-resolution reconstruction of the four-lepton invariant mass.
analysis_objectives.special_considerations: The 7 TeV analysis was re-analysed and combined with 8 TeV data; selections were revised and electron reconstruction/identification improved to enhance low-mass sensitivity.

signal_signatures.signal_process: ggF/VBF/VH production of Higgs boson with H→ZZ(*)→4ℓ
signal_signatures.decay_chain: H→ZZ(*)→4ℓ, where ℓ = e or μ
signal_signatures.event_topology_description: Two reconstructed same-flavour opposite-charge lepton pairs, with one pair consistent with an on-shell Z candidate and the second pair compatible with an off-shell or on-shell Z candidate.
signal_signatures.special_considerations: A Z-mass-constrained kinematic fit is applied to improve m4ℓ resolution; for m4ℓ < 190 GeV the constraint is applied to the leading pair, and for higher masses to both pairs.

background_processes.special_treatment: Estimated from MC simulation normalised to theoretical cross section; m4ℓ-dependent theory uncertainties considered.
Estimated from control regions with transfer factors from MC; treatment differs for ℓℓ+μμ and ℓℓ+ee final states.
background_processes.notes: In low-mass region, leptons arise from heavy-flavour decays, photon conversions, or jet misidentification.

background_processes.special_treatment: Estimated together with Z+jets using dedicated control regions and MC-derived transfer factors; cross-check control samples used.

signal_regions.selection_summary: Quadruplet with leading pair flavour μμ and sub-leading pair μμ; leptons pass trigger, pT thresholds, impact-parameter, isolation, and invariant-mass requirements.
signal_regions.binning: Used as a mass distribution; Figure 2 shows 5 GeV bins for display. Statistical fit binning not_specified.
signal_regions.optimization_strategy: Orthogonal sub-channel split by flavour of leading lepton pair; high-resolution mass reconstruction.
signal_regions.special_considerations: Typical mass resolution at mH=125 GeV is 1.7 GeV.

signal_regions.selection_summary: Quadruplet with leading pair flavour ee and sub-leading pair μμ; common four-lepton selections applied.
signal_regions.binning: Used as a mass distribution; display binning 5 GeV in Figure 2. Statistical fit binning not_specified.
signal_regions.special_considerations: Typical mass resolution at mH=125 GeV is 1.7 GeV.

signal_regions.selection_summary: Quadruplet with leading pair flavour μμ and sub-leading pair ee; common four-lepton selections applied.
signal_regions.binning: Used as a mass distribution; display binning 5 GeV in Figure 2. Statistical fit binning not_specified.
signal_regions.special_considerations: Typical mass resolution at mH=125 GeV is 2.2 GeV.

signal_regions.selection_summary: Quadruplet with leading pair flavour ee and sub-leading pair ee; common four-lepton selections applied.
signal_regions.binning: Used as a mass distribution; display binning 5 GeV in Figure 2. Statistical fit binning not_specified.
signal_regions.special_considerations: Typical mass resolution at mH=125 GeV is 2.3 GeV.

control_regions.selection_summary: For ℓℓ+μμ backgrounds, remove isolation requirement on sub-leading-pair leptons and require at least one sub-leading muon to fail the transverse impact parameter significance selection.
control_regions.justification: Suppresses ZZ(*) contamination and enables simultaneous estimation of ttbar and Z+jets.
control_regions.special_considerations: Transfer factors to the signal region are obtained from MC simulation.

control_regions.selection_summary: Opposite-charge eμ pair with invariant mass 50–106 GeV plus an opposite-charge muon pair; events with a Z candidate decaying to ee or μμ in that mass range are excluded.
control_regions.justification: Cross-check of ttbar background contribution in the ℓℓ+μμ final state.
control_regions.special_considerations: Isolation and transverse impact-parameter significance requirements are applied only to the leptons of the eμ pair.

control_regions.selection_summary: For ℓℓ+ee backgrounds, relax the selection criteria for the electrons of the sub-leading pair and separate electron-background sources with discriminating variables.
control_regions.justification: Allows estimation of the sum of Z+jets and ttbar contributions in the electron-containing reducible background.
control_regions.special_considerations: Uses categories for heavy-flavour leptons, conversion electrons, and jet→electron misidentification.

control_regions.selection_summary: Same-charge sub-leading electron pairs with relaxed selections.
control_regions.justification: Cross-check of the ℓℓ+ee background estimate.
control_regions.special_considerations: An additional same-charge configuration is used where the three highest-pT leptons satisfy all analysis criteria and cuts are relaxed for the remaining electrons.

fit_setup.fit_purpose: Search for a resonant excess in the four-lepton invariant mass spectrum and extract signal significance / signal strength for the H→ZZ(*)→4ℓ channel.
fit_setup.differences_between_fit_versions: Channel is analysed for 7 TeV and 8 TeV separately and in combination; 7 TeV data were re-analysed with improved low-mass sensitivity.
fit_setup.special_considerations: The displayed search observable is m4ℓ. Reducible background normalisations are constrained from control regions, while irreducible ZZ(*) is taken from MC with theory uncertainties.

results.numerical_result: 3.6σ local significance at mH = 125.0 GeV for the combined 7 TeV and 8 TeV H→ZZ(*)→4ℓ channel; expected 2.7σ.
results.dominant_uncertainties: Electron/photon energy scale and resolution matter in the global combination; within this channel, dominant systematics include electron efficiency/scale at low mass and ZZ(*) theory uncertainties.
Experimental lepton efficiencies/scales and theoretical uncertainties on signal and ZZ(*) background.

results.numerical_result: Observed 95% CL exclusion: 131–162 GeV and 170–460 GeV; expected 95% CL exclusion: 124–164 GeV and 176–500 GeV.
results.dominant_uncertainties: Systematic uncertainties incorporated through profile likelihood nuisance parameters.
results.notes: For the H→ZZ(*)→4ℓ channel with combined 7 TeV and 8 TeV data.

results.numerical_result: Within ±5 GeV around 125 GeV: 4μ signal 2.09±0.30, ZZ(*) 1.12±0.05, Z+jets/ttbar 0.13±0.04, observed 6; 2e2μ/2μ2e signal 2.29±0.33, ZZ(*) 0.80±0.05, Z+jets/ttbar 1.27±0.19, observed 5; 4e signal 0.90±0.14, ZZ(*) 0.44±0.04, Z+jets/ttbar 1.09±0.20, observed 2.
results.dominant_uncertainties: Quoted uncertainties combine the uncertainties given for the expected yields in Table 3.
results.notes: Combined 7 TeV and 8 TeV data; window size ±5 GeV around 125 GeV.

analysis_design_insights.description: The analysis is split into 4μ, 2e2μ, 2μ2e, and 4e sub-channels arranged by the flavour of the leading lepton pair.
analysis_design_insights.reason_for_use: Different detector resolutions and reducible-background compositions are handled explicitly.
analysis_design_insights.special_considerations: Background treatment depends strongly on whether the sub-leading pair is μμ or ee.

analysis_design_insights.description: Selections use pT thresholds, impact-parameter significance cuts, track and calorimeter isolation, and low-mass pair vetoes.
analysis_design_insights.reason_for_use: Suppress reducible backgrounds from heavy flavour, conversions, and jet misidentification while keeping high signal purity.
analysis_design_insights.special_considerations: Impact-parameter significance thresholds differ for electrons and muons.

analysis_design_insights.description: Z+jets and ttbar backgrounds are normalised using dedicated control regions with MC-derived transfer factors.
analysis_design_insights.reason_for_use: Reducible backgrounds are difficult to model reliably from simulation alone.
analysis_design_insights.special_considerations: Separate CR definitions are used for ℓℓ+μμ and ℓℓ+ee backgrounds, with multiple cross-checks.

analysis_design_insights.description: A Z-mass constraint is applied to the leading pair below 190 GeV and to both pairs at higher masses.
analysis_design_insights.reason_for_use: Improves the four-lepton invariant-mass resolution and therefore resonance sensitivity.
analysis_design_insights.special_considerations: This is a major driver of the channel's high sensitivity.

sensitivity_drivers.dominant_signal_feature: Narrow resonance in the four-lepton invariant mass spectrum with excellent detector resolution.
sensitivity_drivers.dominant_background_rejection_feature: High-purity isolated prompt-lepton selection plus Z-candidate pairing and impact-parameter/isolation cuts.
sensitivity_drivers.main_limiting_systematics: Electron reconstruction/identification and energy scale uncertainties at low mass, plus theory uncertainties on the irreducible ZZ(*) background.
